Rugby.title: Ospreys' Aussie recruitment drive continues with fly-half signed up
Rugby.summary: The Ospreys have confirmed their fourth new addition for the 26/27 season and their third signing with Australian connections, with Lawson Creighton set to join the club from the NSW Waratahs.
Rugby.body:
The Ospreys have confirmed their fourth new addition for the 26/27 season and their third signing with Australian connections, with Lawson Creighton set to join the club from the NSW Waratahs.

Creighton began his Super Rugby career with the Queensland Reds, making his debut in 2021, before transferring to the Waratahs four years later. The 6’1 tall fly-half, who can also play centre and full-back, made 26 appearances for the ‘Tahs and will provide cover for Wales international Dan Edwards.

Rugby.title: Nick Evans lands new job alongside former All Black
Rugby.summary: Harlequins legend Nick Evans is heading to coach in Japan next season following his departure from the Twickenham Stoop and has agreed a deal to become an assistant coach with Toshiba Brave Lupus.
Rugby.body:
Harlequins legend Nick Evans is heading to coach in Japan next season following his departure from the Twickenham Stoop and has agreed a deal to become an assistant coach with Toshiba Brave Lupus.

The Brave Lupus, who play in Japan Rugby League One’s Division One, were beaten in the quarter-finals of this season’s competition 26-3 by Kubota Spears, who themselves lost the final 22-13 to Kobe Steel earlier this month.

Rugby.title: Where are they now? The sides when Northampton and Exeter last met at Twickenham
Rugby.summary: Northampton Saints and Exeter Chiefs will go head-to-head at Twickenham in the Gallagher PREM Final on Saturday for the right to be called champions of England, but it’s not the first time they have met in a final in South West London.
Rugby.body:
Northampton Saints and Exeter Chiefs will go head-to-head at Twickenham in the Gallagher PREM Final on Saturday for the right to be called champions of England, but it’s not the first time they have met in a final in South West London.

In the first eight years of the new millennium, the Chiefs played in the EDF Energy National Trophy Final four times and lost all four to Rotherham in 2002, Orrell in 2003, Cornish Pirates in 2007, and Saints 24-13 the following season.
Rugby.copy:
Northampton Saints and Exeter Chiefs will go head-to-head at Twickenham in the Gallagher PREM Final on Saturday for the right to be called champions of England, but it’s not the first time they have met in a final in South West London.

In the first eight years of the new millennium, the Chiefs played in the EDF Energy National Trophy Final four times and lost all four to Rotherham in 2002, Orrell in 2003, Cornish Pirates in 2007, and Saints 24-13 the following season.

Rugby.title: Bulls select Bok-heavy team to face Leinster in URC final
Rugby.summary: Willie le Roux and Francois Klopper will each play their 50th BKT URC match for the competition's leading South African team.
Rugby.body:
The Vodacom Bulls have selected 11 Springbok internationals in their starting XV, as they look to put past final disappointments behind them and be crowned champions of the URC.

With opponents Leinster stacked with Ireland players, Friday night’s showpiece match at Croke Park will be like a Test match in all but name. Head coach Johan Ackermann has stuck with the same matchday 23 that shocked Glasgow in the semi-finals with a 22-21 comeback win at Murrayfield a fortnight ago in the hope that they can finally get their hopes on the trophy.

Rugby.title: Leinster make three changes for Bulls URC final
Rugby.summary: Leinster captain Caelan Doris is fit to start Friday's United Rugby Championship final against the Bulls as Tadhg Furlong returns to the line-up.
Rugby.body:
Leinster captain Caelan Doris has been passed fit to start Friday's United Rugby Championship final against the Pretoria Bulls as Tadhg Furlong returns to the line-up. Ireland skipper Doris was a doubt after picking up a knee injury in the semi-final win over the Stormers but he will lead a side showing three changes for the Croke Park decider (19:30 BST).

Fit-again Tommy O'Brien returns on the right wing with James Lowe starting on the opposite side in his final game before departing the province. Hugo Keenan remains at full-back with Rieko Ioane and Jamie Osborne continuing in midfield, while Jamison Gibson-Park and Sam Prendergast are retained in the half-backs.

Rugby.title: Lowe starts in farewell as Leinster get double injury boost for URC final
Rugby.summary: Ireland and Leinster captain Caelan Doris has been passed fit to start in their United Rugby Championship final against the Bulls on Friday at Croke Park.
Rugby.body:
Ireland and Leinster captain Caelan Doris has been passed fit to start in their United Rugby Championship final against the Bulls on Friday at Croke Park. The No.8 was an injury doubt heading into the weekend after sustaining a knee injury in the semi-final victory over the Stormers, and was listed as a player that required further assessment earlier in the week alongside Dan Sheehan, who missed the semi-final with knee and shoulder injuries.

The pair are fit to play in the final, though Sheehan will start on the bench with Ronan Kelleher starting in the front-row. In Andrew Porter’s absence, Jerry Cahir will start at loosehead in his last Leinster appearance before joining Connacht, with Alex Usanov serving as his back-up on the bench.

Rugby.title: Quesada announces Italy squad and explains Ange Capuozzo's absence
Rugby.summary: Italy head coach Gonzalo Quesada has named his 33-man squad for this July's Nations Championship, with three uncapped players included and Ange Capuozzo notably absent.
Rugby.body:
Italy head coach Gonzalo Quesada has named his 33-man squad for this July’s Nations Championship, with three uncapped players included and Ange Capuozzo notably absent. Benetton back-row Giulio Marini, Zebre lock Alessandro Ortombina and Zebre centre Malik Faissal have all received their first senior call-ups, while prop Ion Neculai returns to the Azzurri set-up for the first time since 2022.

Italy open their campaign against Japan in Tokyo on July 4 before daunting clashes with New Zealand in Wellington and Australia in Perth as part of the inaugural Nations Championship.

Rugby.title: League great Sean Long joins Quins coaching staff with ex-England centre
Rugby.summary: Harlequins have continued their coaching overhaul ahead of the 2026/27 season by confirming new roles for former England centre Jordan Turner-Hall and rugby league great Sean Long.
Rugby.body:
Harlequins have continued their coaching overhaul ahead of the 2026/27 season by confirming new roles for former England centre Jordan Turner-Hall and rugby league great Sean Long.

Turner-Hall has been appointed defence and transition coach, extending his 24-year association with the club. The former Quins academy graduate, who earned two caps for England, made 181 appearances during his playing career and was part of the sides that won the Challenge Cup in 2011 and Premiership title in 2012.

Rugby.title: Ojomoh and Caluori to start for England against France XV
Rugby.summary: Max Ojomoh and Noah Caluori will have the chance to press their case for Test starts after being named in the England team to face a France XV on Friday.
Rugby.body:
Max Ojomoh and Noah Caluori will have the chance to press their case for Test starts after being named in the England backline to take on a France XV in a non-cap match in Vannes on Friday.

Centre Ojomoh scored a try and was named player of the match in the win over Argentina in November, but has not played for his country since. Nineteen-year-old Caluori, who has spelled out his ambition to make his England debut this summer, topped the Prem's regular-season try-scoring charts and dominated opponents aerially in a sensational breakout season with Saracens.

Rugby.title: New faces but same Saints DNA
Rugby.summary: Northampton Saints winger Tommy Freeman says the club has the same DNA as their 2024 winning squad despite a much-changed line-up.
Rugby.body:
Two years after winning their second top-flight title, Northampton Saints will return to Twickenham for a shot at a third - though for much of their squad, Saturday's Prem final will be a first.

Of the 23 players in Saints' matchday squad for their thrilling semi-final victory over Leicester Tigers, 13 did not feature in the 2024 final against Bath. Former skipper Courtney Lawes is among eight who played in that final to have exited the club since.

Rugby.title: Quinn Tupaea crowned 2026 Super Rugby Pacific Player of the Year
Rugby.summary: The 2026 Super Rugby Pacific Award winners have been revealed, with star Chiefs midfielder Quinn Tupaea the big winner, named Player of the Year.
Rugby.body:
The 2026 Super Rugby Pacific Award winners have been revealed, with star Chiefs midfielder Quinn Tupaea the big winner, named Player of the Year. The powerful No.12 enters Saturday’s 2026 final with the fifth-most carries in the competition, the most of any back – when counting Leicester Fainga’anuku as a forward – with the sixth-most run metres and ninth-most tries. Defensively, Tupaea has won 15 turnovers in 14 games, the fourth-most of any player, and leads all backline players in tackles completed by a wide margin.

The 27-year-old was crowned via Super Rugby Pacific’s 3-2-1 voting system, where each coach and captain nominates the best (3 points), second-best (2 points), and third-best (1 point) players from the opposing team following the final whistle of each game. This season also saw the introduction of a media panel, which distributed an additional set of 3-2-1 votes among the best players in the game as a whole.

Rugby.title: Blues confirm signing of one-Test Wallaby winger Darby Lancaster
Rugby.summary: The Blues have confirmed the signing of one-time Wallaby Darby Lancaster for the 2027 season.
Rugby.body:
The Blues have confirmed the signing of one-time Wallaby Darby Lancaster for the 2027 season. The 23-year-old is headed for his fourth Super Rugby team after stints with the Rebels, Waratahs, and, most recently, the Force, for whom he started five games in their 2026 campaign, scoring four tries. The New South Wales product claimed his lone Test cap in 2024.

A former U20 star, Lancaster has opted for the unorthodox move in an attempt to grow his game under incoming head coach Jason Holland, who currently oversees the attack portfolio at the Hurricanes in a joint capacity, as he had previously done with the All Blacks.

Rugby.title: Leroy Carter starts as Chiefs lose star rookie for Super Rugby final
Rugby.summary: Chiefs head coach Jono Gibbes has made two changes to his starting XV for the 2026 Super Rugby Pacific final, with two stars of the semi-final win ruled out due to injury.
Rugby.body:
Chiefs head coach Jono Gibbes has made two changes to his starting XV for the 2026 Super Rugby Pacific final, with two stars of the semi-final win ruled out due to injury. Ex-Wallaby Lalakai Foketi and star rookie Isaac Hutchinson will watch the final from the stands, having suffered calf and knee injuries, respectively, in last weekend’s win over the Crusaders.

All Black Leroy Carter and rookie centre Kyle Brown will step up in the injured pair’s absence, assuming the No.14 and No.13 jerseys. It will be Carter’s first start in nearly two months, after making a barnstorming return from a hamstring injury off the bench last time out. Reon Paul comes onto the Chiefs’ bench as Liam Coombes-Fabling shifts to fullback.

Rugby.title: Hurricanes gain one and lose one for Super Rugby Pacific final
Rugby.summary: The Hurricanes have been dealt some good news and some bad news on the injury front heading into this weekend's Super Rugby Pacific final.
Rugby.body:
The Hurricanes have been dealt some good news and some bad news on the injury front heading into this weekend’s Super Rugby Pacific final. The good news is that Devan Flanders is fit and available after missing the last three games through injury, and has been named to start on the blindside flank. The bad news is that Caleb Delany has been ruled out of the contest with a concussion. Isaia Walker-Leawere has been promoted to start in the second row.

Hurricanes head coach Clark Laidlaw has, as a result, opted for no specialist lock cover on the bench, instead backing Brad Shields in the No.19 jersey. The 35-year-old played 11 minutes at lock off the bench against the Brumbies at Super Round, and has had glancing minutes in the second row throughout his 16 years in professional rugby.

Rugby.title: ‘I was content’: James Slipper explains return from Wallabies retirement
Rugby.summary: James Slipper has come out of international retirement for the three home fixtures to start the Nations Championship.
Rugby.body:
James Slipper has come out of international retirement for the three home fixtures to start the Nations Championship, but the Wallabies all-time appearance record-holder is still no guarantee for Men’s Rugby World Cup 2027.

Slipper hung up the international rugby boots after the second Bledisloe Cup Test last year, with the Wallabies falling to the All Blacks in Perth. That was the front-rower’s 151st appearance for the Wallabies, 12 more caps than George Gregan’s career tally.

Rugby.title: Rob Penney on Christian Lio-Willie: 'He deserves another opportunity'
Rugby.summary: Lio-Willie, a qualified dentist, has impressed for the Crusaders during the 2026 Super Rugby Pacific season, pushing his way back into the All Blacks selection frame.
Rugby.body:
Crusaders head coach Rob Penney believes that Christian Lio-Willie deserves another opportunity in the All Blacks, after making three Test appearances under Scott Robertson. Robertson gave the Crusaders loose-forward his All Blacks debut in 2025 against France in Dunedin, where the 27-year-old played the full 80 minutes.

Lio-Willie, a qualified dentist, has impressed for the Crusaders during the 2026 Super Rugby Pacific season, pushing his way back into the All Blacks selection frame. Penney’s Crusaders were knocked out of the 2026 competition last Friday night against the Chiefs in Hamilton, marking the end of the head coach’s era in Christchurch.

Rugby.title: 'Clever footballer': The verdict on Cam Roigard's fake injury tactic against the Blues
Rugby.summary: Hurricanes halfback Cam Roigard has divided opinion after his 'fake injury' tactic against the Blues in the Super Rugby Pacific semi-final.
Rugby.body:
Hurricanes halfback Cam Roigard has divided opinion after his ‘fake injury’ tactic against the Blues in the Super Rugby Pacific semi-final. After taking a high ball contest, Roigard was taken out in the air accidentally in the contest by Blues fullback Beauden Barrett.

As Blues players retreated slowly, with some trying to check on Roigard on the ground, he quickly got to his feet and resumed play with a quick tap off the penalty. The No.9 made a line break with the Blues players not back 10 metres, which eventually led to a try to the Hurricanes.

Rugby.title: ‘Thought it would be the end’: Kaka’s emotional Black Ferns Sevens return
Rugby.summary: This week, the Tokyo Olympics gold medallist fought back tears while announcing she has signed with the Black Ferns Sevens.
Rugby.body:
“Rugby hasn’t seen the end of me,” Shiray Kaka declared in response to a social media edit about 13 months ago. This week, the Tokyo Olympics gold medallist fought back tears while announcing she has signed with the Black Ferns Sevens.

Kaka suffered a devastating knee injury during the 2024 HSBC SVNS Series in Singapore, which ruled the Black Ferns Sevens ace out of the Paris Games. New Zealand claimed gold in front of a sold-out Stade de France crowd, with Kaka supporting from the stands.

Rugby.title: Another SVNS star heads west as Force land rising star Aden Ekanayake
Rugby.summary: Another product of the Australia Sevens program has signed with the Western Force ahead of the 2027 Super Rugby Pacific season.
Rugby.body:
Another product of the Australia Sevens program has signed with the Western Force ahead of the 2027 Super Rugby Pacific season, with Aden Ekanayake set to join teammate Wallace Charlie at the club after agreeing to a three-year deal.

Ekanayake debuted on the HSBC SVNS Series during the early rounds of the 2025 season in Dubai and Cape Town, and it didn’t take the young gun long to emerge as a genuine game-changer and regular standout for the side.

Rugby.title: 'Massive war up front': Why Hurricanes forwards feel they're ready for final
Rugby.summary: The Hurricanes' forwards are preparing for a "massive war up front" in this weekend's Super Rugby Pacific final against their northern neighbours, the Chiefs.
Rugby.body:
The Hurricanes’ forwards are preparing for a “massive war up front” in this weekend’s Super Rugby Pacific final against their northern neighbours, the Chiefs. Both forward packs boasted a bevy of All Blacks in their respective blowout semi-final wins, with the Hurricanes fielding five in addition to England-capped Brad Shiels and Japan captain Warner Dearns, while seven of the eight Chiefs starters were All Blacks and there was one more on the bench.

All that firepower coming down State Highway 1 certainly has fans excited, with the 34,000-seat Hnry Stadium selling out within 15 minutes of tickets going on sale on Tuesday morning.

Rugby.title: Ulster complete triple signing of Bello, Knox and Mullins
Rugby.summary: Ulster complete the signings of props Eduardo Bello and Keynan Knox as well as back Chay Mullins.
Rugby.body:
Ulster have completed the signings of Ireland-qualified prop Keynan Knox and back Chay Mullins. Knox, who is Irish-qualified through residency, has signed a two-year deal to join the province after a spell with French Pro D2 side USON Nevers.

The 27-year-old South African has also played for CS Bourgoin-Jallieu and Munster. He racked up 33 appearances during his six-year stint with Munster, featuring eight times in the United Rugby Championship winning season in 2023.

Rugby.title: Alex Mitchell could make surprise Northampton comeback in Prem final
Rugby.summary: Alex Mitchell could make a surprise comeback in Northampton’s Gallagher Prem final against Exeter on Saturday as he nears recovery from a pulled hamstring.
Rugby.body:
Alex Mitchell could make a surprise comeback in Northampton’s Gallagher Prem final against Exeter on Saturday as he nears recovery from a pulled hamstring. Mitchell was expected to miss the rest of the club season after sustaining the injury while training for England last month, with the July 4 summer tour opener against South Africa earmarked for his return.

Saints were frustrated at losing their British and Irish Lions scrum-half during an international camp staged at the business end of the Prem campaign, six weeks out from the Nations Championship opener in Johannesburg.

Rugby.title: Leinster legend Isa Nacewa gives his take on James Lowe's departure
Rugby.summary: Leinster legend Isa Nacewa admits that he is surprised that Friday’s United Rugby Championship Grand Final against the Bulls will be the last game that James Lowe, their all-time leading try scorer, plays for the club.
Rugby.body:
Leinster legend Isa Nacewa admits that he is surprised that Friday’s United Rugby Championship Grand Final against the Bulls will be the last game that James Lowe, their all-time leading try scorer, plays for the club.

After nine seasons in Irish rugby, Lowe is set to move to Japan, and Nacewa couldn’t be more complimentary about the impact the winger has made. Fijian international full-back Nacewa, who had two spells with Leinster, winning four Heineken Cup titles and two Pro 12/Pro 14 titles, remembers picking Lowe up from Dublin when he first landed at Dublin Airport.

Rugby.title: 'We're trying to avoid that': Erasmus explains surprising selection call
Rugby.summary: South Africa head coach Rassie Erasmus has said that past experiences with injuries influenced his decision to start Quan Horn at fly-half against the Barbarians on Saturday instead of Manie Libbok.
Rugby.body:
South Africa head coach Rassie Erasmus has said that past experiences with injuries influenced his decision to start Quan Horn at fly-half against the Barbarians on Saturday instead of Manie Libbok.

The one-cap Horn, who primarily plays on the wing or at full-back for the Lions, will start at fly-half at the Nelson Mandela Bay Stadium in Gqeberha on Saturday, with 19-year-old Vusi Moyo his back-up on the bench.

Rugby.title: Willie le Roux addresses Jacques Nienaber situation before facing former boss
Rugby.summary: Two-time World Cup winner Willie le Roux says that the Bulls have already laid to rest the ghosts of last season’s United Rugby Championship Grand Final defeat to Leinster, and it will have no bearing on Friday night’s clash.
Rugby.body:
Two-time World Cup winner Willie le Roux says that the Bulls have already laid to rest the ghosts of last season’s United Rugby Championship Grand Final defeat to Leinster, and it will have no bearing on Friday night’s clash.

Le Roux, who was part of the Springboks’ back-to-back World Cup-winning sides in 2019 and 2023, admits that 12 months ago the Bulls never “turned up” at Croke Park and suffered a 32-7 defeat.

Rugby.title: Johan Ackermann: ‘Coaching is a calling. You get the opportunity to influence young men’
Rugby.summary: The Bulls have lost three URC finals in four years, but their head coach is focused on a fresh chance to turn contenders into champions.
Rugby.body:
Johan Ackermann has been here before. Not as coach of the Bulls ahead of a United Rugby Championship final against Leinster in Dublin. That bit is new. But where the details might have changed, the vibe is strangely familiar.

Back in 2016, Ackermann was in charge of the Lions and in three short years had transformed them from Super Rugby outsiders into title contenders. That season, they scored more tries than any other team. They spanked the Crusaders 42-25 in the quarter-finals and then whipped the Highlanders 42-30 a week later to set up a showdown with the Hurricanes in Wellington.

Rugby.title: Gloucester Hartpury add 'world-class' French firepower to 2026/27 squad
Rugby.summary: Experienced France international Gabrielle Vernier will join three-time Premiership Women's Rugby champions Gloucester Hartpury for the 2026/27 season.
Rugby.body:
Experienced France international Gabrielle Vernier will join three-time Premiership Women’s Rugby champions Gloucester Hartpury for the 2026/27 season. The 60-cap centre joins from Blagnac Rugby after a seven year stay with the Toulouse club. During her time with the club, Blagnac were three-time runners-up.

She joins a Gloucester Hartpury team that have missed out on the PWR final for the first time since 2022. Vernier’s impending arrival will act as a tonic for the West Country club, who will see England international Tatyana Heard leave Kingsholm Stadium for Sale Sharks this Summer.

Rugby.title: Gloucester-Hartpury sign France centre Vernier
Rugby.summary: Gloucester-Hartpury sign France centre Gabrielle Vernier for next season from top tier French side Blagnac Rugby Feminin.
Rugby.body:
Gloucester-Hartpury have signed France centre Gabrielle Vernier for the 2026-27 season from top tier French side Blagnac Rugby Feminin. The 29-year-old has been capped 60 times by France since her debut in 2017 and in 2023 was named Six Nations Player of the Championship.

Vernier comes in with England centre Tatyana Heard departing to join Sale Sharks this summer. "From my first conversations with the club, I was struck by the ambition of the programme and the high standards expected every day, which made the opportunity particularly exciting for me," Vernier said.

Rugby.title: No.1 Springboks are third-best in the World Rankings record books
Rugby.summary: The Springboks will go into the inaugural Nations Championship in July sitting proudly at the top of the rankings, with a sizeable 3.61-point gap between themselves and the All Blacks.
Rugby.body:
While South Africa have dominated world rugby since Rassie Erasmus turned things around for the Springboks and led them to back-to-back Rugby World Cup wins in 2019 and 2023, in World Rugby Rankings terms they still have a very long way to go before becoming the most successful team on the planet.

The Springboks will go into the inaugural Nations Championship in July sitting proudly at the top of the rankings, with a sizeable 3.61-point gap between themselves and the All Blacks, who are about to embark on a new era under Dave Rennie.

Rugby.title: Pirates sign prop Clark following Newcastle exit
Rugby.summary: Cornish Pirates sign prop Jamie Clark for the 2026-27 season following his departure from Newcastle Red Bulls.
Rugby.body:
Cornish Pirates have signed prop Jamie Clark for the 2026-27 season following his departure from Newcastle Red Bulls. The 23-year-old joined the Prem side on a short-term deal for the rest of the campaign in April and played in four league games.

Clark was born in Australia and represented the Wallabies at under-20 level as well as playing in the New South Wales Waratahs' academy, but is English-qualified through his father.

Rugby.title: Rassie Erasmus: 'We have been very impressed'
Rugby.summary: South Africa head coach Rassie Erasmus and his coaching team have been "very impressed" by the influx of young players that have been part of the Springboks set-up in recent weeks.
Rugby.body:
South Africa head coach Rassie Erasmus and his coaching team have been “very impressed” by the influx of young players that have been part of the Springboks set-up in recent weeks.

Erasmus named five uncapped players in his first South Africa team of the year to face the Barbarians on Saturday, as well as a further two who only have one cap to their name.

Rugby.title: England's Galligan returns to Quins from Saracens
Rugby.summary: Women's World Cup-winning England lock Rosie Galligan will leave Saracens to rejoin Harlequins at the end of the season.
Rugby.body:
World Cup-winning England lock Rosie Galligan will leave Saracens and rejoin Harlequins at the end of the season. The 28-year-old is currently away from rugby as she is due to give birth in October.

Her partner, fellow England forward Marlie Packer, has already announced a move from Saracens to Harlequins. Galligan first played for Quins between 2021 and 2023, after winning two titles in her first spell at Sarries. After returning to north London, she played more than 50 times for Saracens.

Rugby.title: Zimbabwe call up Stormers lock for South Africa A clash
Rugby.summary: Zimbabwe have included uncapped Stormers lock Gary Porter in their training squad for Saturday's clash against South Africa A at the Nelson Mandela Bay Stadium.
Rugby.body:
Zimbabwe have included uncapped Stormers lock Gary Porter in their training squad for Saturday’s clash against South Africa A at the Nelson Mandela Bay Stadium. Porter, 29, who qualifies for Zimbabwe through his Bulawayo-born mother, has been on the Sables’ radar for some time and has made himself available for the inaugural World Rugby Nations Cup on consecutive weekends in July.

Standing two metres tall and weighing 115kg, Porter will give the Zimbabwe’s tight five some extra ballast, as they prepare to face the Bok second string and then Tonga, USA and Canada in the new gloabl tournament.

Rugby.title: Murphy needs to take stock after semi-final loss
Rugby.summary: Gloucester-Hartpury head coach Dan Murphy says they need time to evaluate how to approach next season.
Rugby.body:
Gloucester-Hartpury head coach Dan Murphy said it will take time to evaluate how to approach next season following their Premiership Women's Rugby (PWR) semi-final defeat to Trailfinders.

The three-times defending champions have dominated the league for the past three campaigns and were unbeaten with 14 straight wins prior to the Women's Six Nations in April. They finished 27 points ahead of their play-off opponents in the table but came into the match off the back of consecutive defeats and with a number of key players absent through injury. They went on to lose 29-26 having trailed 24-7 at half-time.

Rugby.title: Rassie Erasmus names two uncapped players in first Bok XV of 2026
Rugby.summary: South Africa's World Rugby Junior World Championship-winning captain from 2025, second-row Riley Norton, has been named in Rassie Erasmus' starting XV to take on the Barbarians on Saturday at the Nelson Mandela Bay Stadium.
Rugby.body:
South Africa’s World Rugby Junior World Championship-winning captain from 2025, second-row Riley Norton, has been named in Rassie Erasmus’ starting XV to take on the Barbarians on Saturday at the Nelson Mandela Bay Stadium.

The 20-year-old, who captained the Junior Boks to U20 Rugby Championship glory last month, is one of two uncapped players in the starting XV, alongside Bordeaux-Begles’ Investec Champions Cup-winning tighthead prop Carlu Sadie.

Rugby.title: 'I always thought I'd be back': Rosie Galligan's new club revealed
Rugby.summary: Rosie Galligan will return to the Twickenham Stoop ahead of the 2026/27 season for a second spell with Harlequins.
Rugby.body:
Rosie Galligan will return to the Twickenham Stoop ahead of the 2026/27 season for a second spell with Harlequins. The 28-year-old enjoyed a stay with the West London club between 2021 and 2023, during which time she made 30 appearances, but has spent the past three seasons with fellow Premiership Women’s Rugby outfit Saracens, with whom she won two PWR titles with in a previous stay at StoneX Stadium.

She will return to the club as a Women’s Rugby World Cup winner, having played in all six matches of England’s Red Roses‘ triumph last September, and has won 33 caps for her country since her debut six years ago.

Rugby.title: 'This was totally meant to be': Stade Bordelais lock Price loving French adventure
Rugby.summary: Canadian forward Taylor Price has insisted that defending French women’s champions Stade Bordelais understand the scale of the challenge awaiting them at Toulouse in this year’s AXA Elite 1 semi-final.
Rugby.body:
Canadian forward Taylor Price has insisted that defending French women’s champions Stade Bordelais understand the scale of the challenge awaiting them at Toulouse in this year’s AXA Elite 1 semi-final.

“We’ve been building for this semi-final for a while,” she told RugbyPass. “I think our last game we played against Toulouse was a bit of a reference match of what we can do, and we’ve just been building from that.

Rugby.title: Ireland to face South Africa in Cape Town Tests
Rugby.summary: Ireland will travel to South Africa for two Test matches in October as part of the new WXV Global Series.
Rugby.body:
Ireland will travel to South Africa for two Test matches in October as part of the new WXV Global Series. Scott Bemand's side will face the Springbok Women at Athlone Stadium in Cape Town on 24 and 31 October. The only previous meeting between the sides was a 2006 Women's Rugby World Cup match in Canada, which Ireland won 37-0.

The Irish will also host the USA and Japan in the opening games of the series, which is replacing the WXV competition. The fixture against the USA on 20 September will be the first women's Test match held at Dublin's Tallaght Stadium, while Galway's Dexcom Stadium will host the Japan game on 27 September, having been the stage for Ireland's emphatic win over Italy in this year's Six Nations.

Rugby.title: How the Wallabies can still ‘rattle some cages’ at Rugby World Cup 2027
Rugby.summary: Wallabies assistant coach Lauire Fisher believes the team has the potential to “rattle some cages” at Men’s Rugby World Cup 2027.
Rugby.body:
Wallabies assistant coach Laurie Fisher believes the team has the potential to “rattle some cages” at Men’s Rugby World Cup 2027 on home soil, with the Nations Championship shaping up as an important block of Tests.

Fisher will finish up with the Wallabies after the first three Nations Championship Tests, with the first fixture just over two weeks away. Australia hosts Ireland first up at Sydney’s Allianz Stadium, which has already sold out.

Rugby.title: ‘Coming into my prime’: Australia A prop joins Reds from Waratahs
Rugby.summary: Australia A prop Tom Lambert has made the move north of the New South Wales border to the Sunshine State.
Rugby.body:
Australia A prop Tom Lambert has made the move north of the New South Wales border to the Sunshine State, penning a two-year deal with the Queensland Reds. The 25-year-old brings 30 games of Super Rugby Pacific experience from the NSW Waratahs to Ballymore.

Lambert attended Trinity Grammar in Sydney before heading abroad, playing for Scotland U20 and the Glasgow Warriors before returning to Australia’s shores. The front-rower debuted for the Waratahs on February 24, 2023, having been named in the side to play the ACT Brumbies.

Rugby.title: Charlie Cale talks dream All Blacks showdown as Wallabies verdict looms
Rugby.summary: If you look across any international rugby side in the world, the most stacked position talent-wise is usually the backrow, and Australia is no exception.
Rugby.body:
If you look across any international rugby side in the world, the most stacked position talent-wise is usually the backrow, and Australia is no exception. Throughout history, the Wallaby backrow has been home to internationally renowned talent: George Smith, Toutai Kefu, Michael Hooper, David Pocock, and most recently, two-time John Eales Medallist, Rob Valetini.

Other Test sides across the globe also hold elite talent in the No.6, No.7, and No.8 jerseys, and coach Joe Schmidt must pick his very best for the incoming, inaugural Nations Championship in July.

Rugby.title: Chicago Hounds continue unbeaten season with semi-final demolition of Old Glory DC
Rugby.summary: It didn't all go their way though, as Old Glory DC opened the scoring and put the Hounds under pressure during the opening quarter.
Rugby.body:
The Chicago Hounds have confirmed their place in the 2026 Major League Rugby Championship Game on June 28, where they will take on the California Legion. The Championship Game will feature Legion, who overcame the Seattle Seawolves on Monday morning, while the Hounds took care of business, convincingly, 59-22 in Illinois over Old Glory DC.

But a Theo Fourie driving maul five-pointer opened the floodgates as the Hounds scored four unanswered tries to head into the changing rooms with a 28-point lead. It was the underdogs, though, who were on the scoreboard first, through a successful Jason Emery penalty goal from 30 meters out, in front of the posts.

Rugby.title: Wallabies finalise Les Kiss’ coaching staff with key updates
Rugby.summary: The Wallabies coaching staff under incoming boss Les Kiss has been finalised, with Rugby Australia announcing some key updates on Tuesday.
Rugby.body:
The Wallabies coaching staff under incoming boss Les Kiss has been finalised, with Rugby Australia announcing some key updates on Tuesday. Scrum coach John Ulugia will stay on in the role through to the end of 2028, and Jonny Fisher is set to move into the set-up.

Rugby Australia’s Director of High Performance, Peter Horne, expects those two to “bring expertise, stability and continuity” to the Wallabies, as the team continues to build towards Men’s Rugby World Cup 2027 on home soil.

Rugby.title: Rugby Union Weekly: Saints or Chiefs?
Rugby.summary: Who will prevail in Saturday's titanic Prem final meeting between Northampton and Exeter?
Rugby.body: Have Exeter shown enough to convince us they can beat Northampton in Saturday’s Prem final? Ugo, Chris and Ashy look back at both semi-finals and assess whether the Chiefs have the game to upset the favourites. Or are Northampton simply operating on another level? Plus, with Steve Borthwick naming his 33-man squad to face a France XV on Friday, they debate whether Ollie Lawrence can count himself unlucky to miss out.

Rugby.title: Rising star re-signs with Force after Super Rugby Pacific breakthrough
Rugby.summary: Australia Under-20s international Boston Fakafanua has re-signed with the Western Force for another two seasons.
Rugby.body:
Australia Under-20s international Boston Fakafanua has re-signed with the Western Force for another two seasons, as the utility back looks to build on a maiden appearance in Super Rugby Pacific last month.

Fakafanua debuted at Super Rugby level against the Fijian Drua on May 23, playing eight minutes off the bench after replacing Wallaby Hamish Stewart. The Force held on for a nail-biting 19-15 win, with former NRL star Zac Lomax crossing for a late try at Perth’s HBF Park.

Rugby.title: Commentator weighs in on All Blacks No.10 debate ahead of final
Rugby.summary: "But that's something that still has to be proven, his ability to produce that at test level, but I thought he was fantastic on Friday night."
Rugby.body:
Sky Sport commentator Tony Johnson has weighed in on this weekend’s matchup between Damian McKenzie and Ruben Love in the Super Rugby Pacific final. The matchup between the likely starting first-fives could have a direct impact on who All Blacks head coach Dave Rennie selects as his first choice option for the first Nations Championship Test against France.

McKenzie, who brought up his 150th Chiefs appearance on Friday night, played a pivotal role in his side’s impressive 40-minute demolition of the Crusaders in the semi-final. But the Hurricanes first-five has starred in the most in-form backline in Super Rugby Pacific, growing into his role as a primary game-driver alongside All Black teammates Jordie Barrett and Cameron Roigard.

Rugby.title: Highlanders begin off-season with five re-signings
Rugby.summary: The Highlanders haven't wasted any time in ticking into off-season mode, announcing five re-signings as the club's fresh-faced potential starts to make its mark in Super Rugby Pacific.
Rugby.body:
The Highlanders haven’t wasted any time in ticking into off-season mode, announcing five re-signings as the club’s fresh-faced potential starts to make its mark in Super Rugby Pacific.

Jamie Joseph’s youthful revolution in Dunedin has given the club one of the most exciting young cores in the competition, and four of the new contracts signed are two-year extensions, keeping that core together.

Rugby.title: Wellington set for packed house as Super Rugby final sells out in 15 minutes
Rugby.summary: "To see the Grand Final sell out so quickly is a fantastic result for our competition, the clubs and our fans," Mesley said.
Rugby.body:
Tickets went on sale to the public at 10am NZT on Tuesday morning, after members’ pre-sale tickets on Sunday evaporated within hours. A crowd of 34,500 is expected into the “cake tin”, to witness the first Super Rugby Pacific final in Wellington since the Hurricanes’ 2016 triumph over the Lions.

The final will be played at Hnry Stadium in Wellington at 7:05pm NZT on Saturday evening, between the two highest-ranked teams from the regular season. The Chiefs provided their fans with a standout performance against the Crusaders last Friday, showcasing the quality of their side in front of a sold-out FMG Stadium Waikato crowd.

Rugby.title: Australia U20 fly-half turns down another club's offer to re-sign with Reds
Rugby.summary: Australia Under-20s playmaker Finn Mackay has knocked back an offer from another Super Rugby Pacific club to stay in the Sunshine State.
Rugby.body:
Australia Under-20s playmaker Finn Mackay has knocked back an offer from another Super Rugby Pacific club to stay in the Sunshine State, re-signing with the Queensland Reds on a two-year deal through 2028.

Mackay touched down for two tries in as many matches for the age-grade Australian side during the recent U20 Rugby Championship in South Africa. The fly-half has just been named in a strong squad for the World Rugby Junior World Championship in Georgia from June 27 to July 18.

Rugby.title: PWR semi-finals: Talking points as Trailfinders and Saracens reached the Final
Rugby.summary: After 74 matches of the 2025/26 Premiership Women's Rugby season just one more fixture remains.
Rugby.body:
After 74 matches of the 2025/26 Premiership Women’s Rugby season just one more fixture remains. The 75th match of the campaign will see Trailfinders Women and Saracens go toe-to-toe at the Twickenham Stoop in a winner-takes all finale.

Trailfinders’ 29-26 win against Gloucester Hartpury ended the West Country club’s three-year dominance of English club rugby with the second-ever away win in a PWR semi-final, while it took a late Jess Breach try to help Saracens to a 40-38 win over Exeter Chiefs at StoneX Stadium.
Rugby.copy:
After 74 matches of the 2025/26 Premiership Women’s Rugby season just one more fixture remains. The 75th match of the campaign will see Trailfinders Women and Saracens go toe-to-toe at the Twickenham Stoop in a winner-takes all finale.

Trailfinders’ 29-26 win against Gloucester Hartpury ended the West Country club’s three-year dominance of English club rugby with the second-ever away win in a PWR semi-final, while it took a late Jess Breach try to help Saracens to a 40-38 win over Exeter Chiefs at StoneX Stadium.

Rugby.title: Henry Pollock looked like the traditional No.8 England need: Andy Goode
Rugby.summary: Henry Pollock isn’t just proving his doubters wrong, he’s making them look downright daft after a world class performance in his first ever Prem play-off.
Rugby.body:
Henry Pollock isn’t just proving his doubters wrong, he’s making them look downright daft after a world class performance in his first ever Prem play-off. Everyone knows about his force of personality and that’s the main reason for the criticism he’s received but it’s also a major factor behind his ability to perform on the biggest stages and he should be judged on the levels he’s producing on the field.

If you do that, I don’t think anyone can argue he’s a superstar already and he may only be 21 years of age but the next step is getting a consistent run in the England team at one of the most attritional positions in number eight.

Rugby.title: Leinster prop Porter ruled out of URC final
Rugby.summary: Ireland prop Andrew Porter has been ruled out of Leinster's United Rugby Championship final with the Bulls at Croke Park.
Rugby.body:
Ireland prop Andrew Porter has been ruled out of Leinster's United Rugby Championship final with the Bulls. Porter was forced off in the first half of the semi-final win over the Stormers and will miss Friday's decider at Croke Park.

Captain Caelan Doris and hooker Dan Sheehan are also both injury doubts and will be assessed before the game. Doris picked up a knee injury in the win over the Stormers while Sheehan missed the last-four tie.

Rugby.title: Prem talking points: Bath's missing drop-goal, Feyi-Waboso's shower & Pollock's revenge
Rugby.summary: A captivating pair of Prem semi-finals featured drama, defiance and thrown drinks in the stands as Exeter and Northampton prevailed.
Rugby.body:
It's important to remember that Finn Russell doesn't like dropping goals either. Bath's first-choice fly-half famously prefers keeping ball in hand, having landed only one in open play in his entire playing career.

That one though came at the exact place that Santi Carreras, standing in for the injured Russell, turned down a string of opportunities at the death in the 27-26 defeat by Exeter on Sunday.

Rugby.title: Sale Sharks leaver Hyron Andrews to return to South Africa
Rugby.summary: Sale Sharks lock Hyron Andrews is returning to South Africa to join the Lions after the United Rugby Championship outfit qualified for the Champions Cup next season.
Rugby.body:
Sale Sharks lock Hyron Andrews is returning to South Africa to join the Lions after the United Rugby Championship outfit qualified for the Champions Cup next season. The Gallagher Prem side announced a month ago that Andrews would be one of 20 players who would be leaving the club when their contracts run out at the end of the month in a massive clear-out of their bloated squad.

Andrews, 30, spent eight years with the Durban-based Sharks before moving to Greater Manchester two years ago, where he made 45 appearances, including 19 in all competitions, in the season that has just ended.

Rugby.title: Scotland aim to create 'strongest possible future' with player contract expansion
Rugby.summary: Scottish Rugby’s plan to have the majority of the country’s top female players plying their trade north of the border going forward is now well and truly underway after the governing body revealed their contracting model for 2026/27.
Rugby.body:
Scottish Rugby’s plan to have the majority of the country’s top female players plying their trade north of the border going forward is now well and truly underway after the governing body revealed their contracting model for 2026/27.

In the 2025/26 contracting period 35 players were under contract, but just 18 of them based in Scotland. Fast forward to now, and Scottish Rugby’s head of women’s performance and pathways Andy Rhys Jones has announced that 47 players in total will be contracted in the coming season. This will include 35 based at ‘home’ and linked to either Edinburgh Rugby or Glasgow Warriors.

Rugby.title: ‘Selection is everything. Borthwick’s England needs to reflect the character of the league the players are drawn from’
Rugby.summary: England need to tailor their selection to reflect how players are used to at their Prem teams... and 'add a little taste of spicy HP sauce' for good measure.
Rugby.body:
As the game clock ticked down slowly to zero at the old Boston Garden and the fans stamped in the aisles and chanted the countdown, a curl of blue smoke would inevitably begin to rise in a widening gyre above the wooden parquet floor. When his beloved Boston Celtics were marching towards yet another NBA championship, ‘Red’ Auerbach was famous for lighting up his victory cigar before proceedings had even finished.

It was not so much a sign of arrogance as a statement of vision, because ‘Red’ was always one step ahead of the game, as a coach or a general manager. He won 16 titles in a 29-year span and was one of the game’s great pioneers. He first developed the idea of the ‘fast break’ offence and the importance of ‘the sixth man’ as a game-changer off the bench. He picked the first all African-American starting five in 1964 and appointed Bill Russell as the first black coach in the NBA two years later.

Rugby.title: Report: URC finalists Leinster and Bulls suffer from fan fatigue
Rugby.summary: An extensive report by The Rugby Paper into the regular-season attendances in Europe's three main leagues shows Bordeaux-Begles way out in front as the best-supported team.
Rugby.body:
An extensive report by The Rugby Paper on the regular-season attendances in Europe’s three main leagues shows Bordeaux-Begles way out in front as the best-supported team. The back-to-back Investec Champions Cup winners may have fallen short of the Top 14 play-offs this year, but their average attendance is now touching 35,000 (34, 578), which represents a five per cent increase on the year before.

Bordeaux were one of six clubs out of the 40 that make up the Top 14, United Rugby Championship and the Gallagher PREM to achieve a 20,000+ average crowd. Next-best were The Stormers (26,153), followed by Toulouse (21,836), Bristol (21,347), Leinster (20,789) and, helped by their Big Game outing at The Allianz Stadium, Harlequins (20, 423).

Rugby.title: Chile and Martín Sigren begin to dream of making Rugby World Cup history
Rugby.summary: This summer's Nations Cup will give Chile valuable time together ahead of their bid to make the knockout stages at next year's World Cup
Rugby.body:
In the crisp Andean sunshine, Chile’s Super Rugby Americas franchise Selknam are wrapping up a training session. In the middle of the group, Martín Sigren offers a few final words before the players disperse. It is impossible to make out what he is saying, but it is clear that every member of the group is hanging on his every word. After missing nearly a year of action due to a serious knee injury, Chile’s captain is back in his element.

Having led Los Condores to their maiden World Cup in 2023, he had to watch last year’s qualification for the 2027 tournament from the sideline. With a decade of international rugby behind him, his time away from the pitch has given him a chance to reflect on how much has changed since his debut in 2016.

Rugby.title: South Africa and Ireland to play for first time in 20 years at WXV Global Series
Rugby.summary: The Springbok Women have confirmed they will host a first-ever Test series against Ireland in October as part of the WXV Global Series.
Rugby.body:
The Springbok Women have confirmed they will host a first-ever Test series against Ireland in October as part of the WXV Global Series. Fixtures will take place on the 24 and 31 October at Athlone Stadium in Cape Town. It will be the first time that the teams have met in 20 years.

To date Ireland and South Africa have only ever met once before. Ireland won that fixture 37-0 in Edmonton at the Women’s Rugby World Cup in 2006. At this year’s Guinness Women’s Six Nations, Ireland finished third in the tournament.

Rugby.title: Exeter's play-off run forces Hooper 'to grow'
Rugby.summary: Exeter's Australia flanker Tom Hooper says his first season at Sandy Park has forced him to grow as a player.
Rugby.body:
Exeter's Australia flanker Tom Hooper has said his first season at Sandy Park has forced him to grow as a player. Hooper, 25, helped the Chiefs reach their first Prem final in five years as they won a nail-biting semi-final 27-26 at Bath on Saturday.

It was Hooper's 22nd appearance for Exeter since joining the club last autumn. "It's challenged me to grow, certainly. Not only on the field, off the field as well," he told BBC Sport after the win.

Rugby.title: Champions Cup winners dominate France squad to face England XV
Rugby.summary: Fabien Galthie has named his 28-man France squad that will face an England XV on Friday in Vannes, live on RPTV, with nine players from European champions Bordeaux-Begles.
Rugby.body:
Fabien Galthie has named his 28-man France squad that will face an England XV on Friday in Vannes, live on RPTV, with nine players from European champions Bordeaux-Begles. Galthie has taken advantage of Bordeaux’s failure to make the Top 14 play-offs, and has flooded his squad with their players, including a return for loosehead Jefferson Poirot. There is, however, no place in the squad for stars Maxime Lucu, Matthieu Jalibert and Louis Bielle-Biarrey for the uncapped fixture.

After losing in the Top 14 quarter-finals at the weekend, both Pau and La Rochelle are well-represented in the squad, although Gregory Alldritt is still absent after being left out of France’s Guinness Six Nations squad.

Rugby.title: 14 uncapped players included in Wales squad for Barbarians clash
Rugby.summary: Wales head coach Sean Lynn has named a 33-player squad for his team's clash with the Barbarians on Saturday 27 June at Allianz Stadium.
Rugby.body:
Wales head coach Sean Lynn has named a 33-player squad for his team’s clash with the Barbarians on Saturday 27 June at Allianz Stadium. In total there are 14 players that could wear a senior Wales jersey for the first time in the uncapped fixture. This includes teenage scrum-half Lily Hawkins, GB Sevens’ Hanna Tudor and prop forward Crystal James.

They will be joined in the squad by senior internationals Alisha and Jasmine Joyce, who last week confirmed that they will play their domestic rugby back in Wales from next season.

Rugby.title: Ojomoh comes into England squad to face France XV
Rugby.summary: Max Ojomoh could line up alongside midfield rival Benhard Janse van Rensburg in England's non-cap match against a France XV in Vannes on Friday evening.
Rugby.body:
Max Ojomoh could line up alongside midfield rival Benhard Janse van Rensburg in England's non-cap match against a France XV in Vannes on Friday evening, after he was called into a 33-strong squad for the fixture.

However, there is no recall for Ojomoh's Bath's colleague Ollie Lawrence, who is overlooked once again. Lawrence is one of England's 25 centrally-contracted players and started the autumn win over New Zealand.

Rugby.title: Callender back as Wales call up new faces
Rugby.summary: The 25-year-old missed Wales' Women's Six Nations campaign because of injury, but is set to return against Barbarians on 27 June.
Rugby.body:
Wales have been boosted by the return from injury of highly-regarded back-row Alex Callender for their uncapped encounter against Barbarians at Allianz Stadium in Twickenham on Saturday, 27 June.

The 25-year-old missed Wales' Women's Six Nations campaign because of injury, but is set to return for a fixture that follows a men's international against the Barbarians in a double-header at the home of English rugby.

Rugby.title: England call up 13 losing PREM semi-finalists including previously snubbed star
Rugby.summary: Bath centre Max Ojomoh has been recalled to Steve Borthwick's England squad as one of 13 players from losing Gallagher PREM semi-finalists Leicester Tigers and Bath that will prepare to take on a France XV on Friday in Vannes, live on RPTV.
Rugby.body:
Bath centre Max Ojomoh has been recalled to Steve Borthwick’s England squad as one of 13 players from losing Gallagher PREM semi-finalists Leicester Tigers and Bath that will prepare to take on a France XV on Friday in Vannes, live on RPTV, but there is still no place again for captain Maro Itoje.

The two-cap Ojomoh, who was a surprise omission from Borthwick’s England squad named last month for a three-day camp, is one of five Bath players in the 33-man squad, which includes seven uncapped players. The other four members are all in the pack – loosehead Beno Obano, uncapped tighthead Billy Sela, lock Charlie Ewels and lock/ back-row Ted Hill.

Rugby.title: Keiron Assiratti withdrawn from Wales squad
Rugby.summary: Cardiff tighthead prop Keiron Assiratti will miss Wales' Nations Championship campaign in July due to a calf injury.
Rugby.body:
Cardiff tighthead prop Keiron Assiratti will miss Wales’ Nations Championship campaign in July due to a calf injury. The 28-year-old has been withdrawn from Steve Tandy’s squad after picking up the injury in Cardiff’s United Rugby Championship quarter-final defeat to the Stormers.

Wales face the Barbarians at Twickenham’s Allianz Stadium on June 27, before embarking on a Nations Championship series which will see them face Fiji in Cardiff, Argentina in San Juan and South Africa in Durban in consecutive weekends in July.

Rugby.title: Israel women’s rugby sevens team claims bronze at European championship in Andorra
Rugby.summary: Israel women’s rugby sevens team claims bronze at European championship in Andorra Ynetnews
Rugby.body:
Israel’s women’s rugby sevens team finished third out of 11 nations at the Rugby Europe Women’s Sevens Conference Championship in Andorra, defeating Slovakia 14-12 in the bronze-medal match.

The Israeli squad recovered from a difficult start after losing to Finland in the pool stage, then responded with decisive victories over Luxembourg, 46-0, and Bosnia and Herzegovina, 45-5, to secure a place in the knockout rounds.

Rugby.title: Wales prop Assiratti ruled out of summer series
Rugby.summary: Prop Keiron Assiratti has been ruled out of Wales' summer matches because of a calf injury.
Rugby.body:
Prop Keiron Assiratti has been ruled out of Wales' summer matches because of a calf injury. The Cardiff tight-head suffered the injury during the United Rugby Championship (URC) quarter-final play-off defeat against Stormers at the end of May.

It is a blow for head coach Steve Tandy who was already without Tomas Francis, who is being rested this summer, while Bath's Archie Griffin is missing after undergoing surgery.

Rugby.title: Top 14 escape artists Perpignan live to fight another day
Rugby.summary: Relief for Perpignan and disappointment for Philippe Saint-Andre's Provence as the Catalan club retain their place in the Top 14.
Rugby.body:
For the fifth time in a row, USA Perpignan have managed to avoid relegation in the barrage play-off, having clinched their place in next year’s Top 14 after a 47-24 win over Provence at Stade Maurice-David.

Under a blazing hot sun and a tense but colourful atmosphere, both sides went chasing for a way to get into the driver’s seat, but neither was able to find a way to score points in the first quarter.

Rugby.title: Legion beat Seawolves in 77-point thriller to book MLR Championship berth
Rugby.summary: California Legion have punched their ticket to the 2026 Major League Rugby Championship game, beating Seattle Seawolves.
Rugby.body:
California Legion have punched their ticket to the 2026 Major League Rugby Championship game in their inaugural season as a new franchise, overcoming a valiant Seattle Seawolves outfit 43-34 in a high-scoring semi-final.

MLR announced last July that San Diego Legion and Rugby Football Club Los Angeles had agreed in principle to come together. The Legion finished the regular season in third with a 5-5 record, which included a recent defeat to the Seawolves.

Rugby.title: Former All Blacks highlight three players demanding Dave Rennie's attention
Rugby.summary: The Sky Sport Breakdown panel of Jeff Wilson, Mils Muliaina, and Stephen Donald all picked one player that has stood out in recent weeks, staking their claim for Rennie's first All Blacks squad.
Rugby.body:
With Dave Rennie’s first All Blacks squad under seven days away from being named, three former All Blacks have discussed three players who are timing their run to perfection. Rennie, his coaching staff, and NZR Chair David Kirk will name the 34-man squad for the three Nations Championship fixtures in New Zealand starting on July 4 against France.

The Sky Sport Breakdown panel of Jeff Wilson, Mils Muliaina, and Stephen Donald all picked one player that has stood out in recent weeks, staking their claim for Rennie’s first All Blacks squad.

Rugby.title: Blitzboks duo reflect on World Championship triumph after Bordeaux scare
Rugby.summary: Shilton van Wyk and David Brits have expressed immense gratitude and pride after winning the 2026 HSBC SVNS World Championship.
Rugby.body:
Shilton van Wyk and David Brits have expressed immense gratitude and pride after winning the 2026 HSBC SVNS World Championship, with the Blitzboks surviving a scare in Bordeaux to secure the circuit’s top prize.

New Zealand, South Africa and Fiji each took out one event in the early stages of the regular season before the Blitzboks began to take over. South Africa won another four titles to take out the SVNS Series and carried that dominance into the new-look World Championship.

Rugby.title: Rising star Harry McLaughlin-Phillips among five farewelled by Reds
Rugby.summary: Rising star Harry McLaughlin-Phillips is set for a new chapter elsewhere after the Queensland Reds confirmed the departure of five players.
Rugby.body:
Rising star Harry McLaughlin-Phillips is set for a new chapter elsewhere after the Queensland Reds confirmed the departure of five players, with that group boasting more than 170 games for the club between them.

The Reds have thanked former All Blacks prop Jeffery Toomaga-Allen, hooker Richie Asiata, halfback Louis Werchon, outside back Jeremaia Murray and McLaughlin-Phillips for their efforts over the years at Ballymore.

Rugby.title: 'I don’t think I was milking it': Cameron Roigard reacts to quick-tap incident
Rugby.summary: Speaking to Jason Pine on Weekend Sports on Newstalk ZB, Roigard explained the play from his perspective.
Rugby.body:
All Blacks halfback Cameron Roigard has responded to social media discussion around his incident with Blues first-five Beauden Barrett. Roigard was taken out in the air by Barrett while taking a high-ball catch during the first half, before the halfback took the opportunity to take a quick tap and burst through a gap in the Blues’ defence.

The Hurricanes halfback was influential in their 57-21 victory over Vern Cotter’s Blues on Saturday night, clinching the side’s first Super Rugby Pacific final appearance since the 2016 grand final against the Lions.

Rugby.title: ‘The hurt is real’: Black Ferns Sevens stars reflect on SVNS heartbreak
Rugby.summary: Black Ferns Sevens captain Risi Pouri-Lane says “the hurt is real” after the team’s heartbreaking 26-19 defeat to Australia in Bordeaux.
Rugby.body:
Black Ferns Sevens captain Risi Pouri-Lane says “the hurt is real” after the team’s heartbreaking 26-19 defeat to Australia in Bordeaux, which saw them fall short of the 2026 HSBC SVNS World Championship crown.

Kelsey Teneti and Mahina Paul are among the other Black Ferns Sevens players who have penned meaningful messages on social media in the week since the season finale, which ended with back-to-back tournaments in Europe.

Rugby.title: Waratahs set up Super W win with incredible first half
Rugby.summary: NSW got off to a flying start and led 26-7 at halftime, with Wallaroos lock Leaney chiming in with two tries in her 25th match for her side.
Rugby.body:
NSW have maintained their hold on their Super W rivals, with Kaitlan Leaney scoring two tries in a dominant first half as the Waratahs banked a 29-17 victory over the Brumbies.

The reigning champions have never been beaten by a domestic rival and kept the impressive record alive at Canberra’s Viking Park in their Sunday clash. NSW got off to a flying start and led 26-7 at halftime, with Wallaroos lock Leaney chiming in with two tries in her 25th match for her side.

Rugby.title: 'It would mean everything': Packer eyes perfect Saracens exit
Rugby.summary: Marlie Packer wants Saracens to end Trailfinders Women's party at the Premiership Women's Rugby Final in two weeks' time.
Rugby.body:
Marlie Packer wants Saracens to end Trailfinders Women’s party at the Premiership Women’s Rugby Final in two weeks’ time. The 36-year-old England international, who will join Harlequins at seasons end, helped her team reach a sixth PWR Final on Sunday afternoon as the North London side dug deep to beat Exeter Chiefs 40-38 at StoneX Stadium.

It was Jess Breach’s second try of the game in the 74th minute that ultimately sent Packer and her teammates to the finale, where they will face a Trailfinders team that beat back-to-back-to-back English champions Gloucester Hartpury 29-26 earlier in the day.

Rugby.title: Match-winning Jess Breach try sends Saracens to sixth PWR Final
Rugby.summary: Jess Breach's late try for Saracens set up an all-London clash in the Premiership Women's Rugby Final with Trailfinders Women.
Rugby.body:
Jess Breach’s late try for Saracens set up an all-London clash in the Premiership Women’s Rugby Final with Trailfinders Women. The England international’s try in the 74th minute helped the North London side to a 40-38 win over Exeter Chiefs at StoneX Stadium and to their fifth PWR final.

By full-time Breach had scored a brace alongside teammate Olivia Apps, while Alysha Corrigan and Donna Rose had also made their way over the whitewash. In a fortnight’s time, at the Twickenham Stoop, Saracens will aim to end a four-year hiatus as English champions.

Rugby.title: How Meg Jones inspired Trailfinders to semi-final success against Gloucester Hartpury
Rugby.summary: Meg Jones wryly smiled when asked by TNT Sports' Sarra Elgan who she wanted to play in the Premiership Women's Rugby Final.
Rugby.body:
Meg Jones wryly smiled when asked by TNT Sports’ Sarra Elgan who she wanted to play in the Premiership Women’s Rugby Final. England’s stand-in captain was freshly removed from inspiring Trailfinders Women’s 29-26 semi-final victory over Gloucester Hartpury. The 29-year-old was a try-scorer in the fixture and even provided the final touch for Emma Uren’s try. She was even influential in Isla Norman-Bell’s second effort of the game.

It was the first knockout game of rugby that Trailfinders have ever played. They take a place in the PWR final at the Twickenham Stoop in a fortnight’s time as just the second team to claim an away win in a PWR semi-final.

Rugby.title: Trailfinders stun Gloucester-Hartpury to reach PWR final
Rugby.summary: Trailfinders hold off Gloucester-Hartpury as they beat reigning champions 29-26 to reach their first Premiership Women's Rugby final.
Rugby.body:
Trailfinders held off Gloucester-Hartpury as they beat the reigning champions 29-26 to reach their first Premiership Women's Rugby (PWR) final. They will play Saracens for the title on Sunday, 28 June, who emerged as narrow 40-38 winners against Exeter Chiefs in the other semi-final.

Hosts Gloucester-Hartpury had won the title for the past three seasons and took the lead at Kingsholm - rebadged as Queensholm for the PWR play-offs - through a Tatyana Heard try.

Rugby.title: Trailfinders end Gloucester Hartpury's 'four-peat' ambition with stunning Queensholm win
Rugby.summary: Trailfinders Women stunned Gloucester Hartpury to take a place in the Premiership Women's Rugby final for the first time.
Rugby.body:
Trailfinders Women stunned Gloucester Hartpury to take a place in the Premiership Women’s Rugby final for the first time. The West London club claimed just the second-ever away victory in a PWR semi-final to secure their spot at the Twickenham Stoop in a fortnight’s time. It is the second time that Barney Maddison’s team have beaten Gloucester Hartpury in two weeks and have ended the West Country club’s hopes of a fourth English league title in a row this season with the 29-26 victory.

This contest had a little bit of everything at a ravenous Queensholm Stadium. Gloucester Hartpury tried their best to overturn a 17-point half-time deficit as tries from Maya Monteil, Meg Jones Isla Norman-Bell and Emma Uren gave Trailfinders a 24-7 lead at the break.

Rugby.title: Fin Smith highlights key Henry Pollock factor fuelling Northampton
Rugby.summary: Fin Smith insists Henry Pollock is energising Northampton’s Gallagher Prem title march through his “larger than life character”.
Rugby.body:
Fin Smith insists Henry Pollock is energising Northampton’s Gallagher Prem title march through his “larger than life character”. Saints will collide with Exeter in Saturday’s final at Allianz Stadium following two gripping play-offs that saw Leicester and Bath bow out with their pride intact.

Smith was named man of the match against Tigers with Steve Borthwick watching at Franklin’s Gardens and the England head coach will also have been impressed with a seismic contribution from Pollock.

Rugby.title: 'You’ve set the precedent': Matatu begin Super Rugby Aupiki season with record crowd
Rugby.summary: Matatu kicked off the new Super Rugby Aupiki season with a record attendance for a standalone domestic women's rugby fixture on Saturday.
Rugby.body:
Matatu kicked off the new Super Rugby Aupiki season with a record attendance for a standalone domestic women’s rugby fixture on Saturday. Ahead of the fixture, the Christchurch team confirmed that 10,000 tickets had been sold for the 2026 season opener at One New Zealand Stadium.

This was more than double the record set in 2024 when the Blues hosted Chiefs Manawa at Eden Park when 4,800 supporters made their way to the venue. By the time Matatu’s game against the Chiefs Manawa got underway there were 11,793 supporters surrounding the field.

Rugby.title: Saints and Chiefs headline Gallagher PREM semifinal Team of the week
Rugby.summary: For the seventh consecutive season, the Gallagher PREM will crown a new champion when the Northampton Saints and Exeter Chiefs clash on Jun 20th at Allianz Stadium Twickenham.
Rugby.body:
For the seventh consecutive season, there will be no back-to-back Gallagher PREM champions, with Northampton Saints and Exeter Chiefs set to decide the title at Allianz Stadium, Twickenham on June 20.

After a semifinal weekend loaded with rivalry, momentum swings and late drama, the final is now set. First came Friday night at Franklin’s Gardens, where Northampton and Leicester traded blows in a Midlands derby that delivered 12 tries and barely paused for breath. It was only in the 74th minute that Saints finally broke clear, with captain George Furbank racing away for the score that put the contest beyond Leicester.

Rugby.title: Moody starts 500-mile cycle after MND diagnosis
Rugby.summary: The former England Rugby captain, aged 48, was diagnosed with Motor Neurone Disease last year.
Rugby.body:
England's former rugby captain Lewis Moody is embarking on a 500-mile (804km) bike ride to raise awareness around Motor Neurone Disease (MND) following his own diagnosis. The 48-year-old, along with more than a dozen rugby players, friends and family, set off from Newcastle Red Bulls' stadium for Rugby HQ in Twickenham.

Moody paid tribute to George "Doddie" Weir, who played for the Red Bulls and died from MND in 2022. He said the efforts of MND campaigners such as Kevin Sinfield meant there was hope that the discovery of a cure was getting closer.

Rugby.title: One that got away
Rugby.summary: Bath boss Johann van Graan calls their Prem semi-final defeat by Exeter "one that got away" as they miss out on a return to Twickenham.
Rugby.body:
The Rolling Stones' 'You Can't Always Get What You Want' was blaring out of the speakers at the Recreation Ground as Bath players stood on the pitch in disbelief after Saturday's agonising semi-final defeat by Exeter.

A year ago, Bath were revelling at Twickenham in a first Prem title in 29 years that also completed a historic hat-trick of trophies for the season. Head of rugby Johann van Graan spoke about how champion sides are "teams that win things multiple times" and, after finishing second in the table this campaign, a third successive Prem final appearance and shot at retaining the title was meant to be in the script.

Rugby.title: Slade made Exeter believe in final return
Rugby.summary: Exeter boss Rob Baxter says Henry Slade's leadership has been key to his side reaching the Prem final.
Rugby.body:
Exeter boss Rob Baxter says Henry Slade's leadership has been key to his side reaching the Prem final. The 33-year-old England centre has been impressive in recent weeks - leading Baxter to describe him as in "world class form".

Slade was sin-binned for a deliberate knock-on in the first half of their semi-final at Bath on Saturday. But he returned to help orchestrate a historic fightback as the Chiefs overturned a 26-10 deficit to win 27-26 and become the first third-placed side to make a Prem final.

Rugby.title: Andy Farrell sticks around as uncertainty swirls around Irish rugby
Rugby.summary: The departure of James Lowe signifies a change of tack from within Irish rugby as developing academy starlets is preferred
Rugby.body:
Simon Easterby will have to bide his time a while longer. Paul O’Connell can cool his jets. Stuart Lancaster can stay out West. Ronan O’Gara may have to find a Test gig somewhere else. Andy Farrell is sticking around with Ireland until after the 2031 World Cup. At a time when much uncertainty is swirling around Irish rugby, the IRFU has moved quick to keep the national team steady.

Mack Hansen went too far with claims, on the Kick Offs and Kick Ons podcast, Ireland’s sporting media was baying for Farrell’s head, after the Six Nations loss to France. Hansen was accurate, though, when he spoke of a ‘pretty big media explosion, just on negativity’. France outclassing Ireland in Paris came on the heels of November losses to New Zealand and South Africa, when the hosts clung on for dear life as scrum after scrum was obliterated.

Rugby.title: Du'Plessis Kirifi weighs in on Hurricanes’ inexperience ahead of final
Rugby.summary: "It's a great question, for 99.999% of the group it's a new feeling. I think we've got enough experience within the group," Kirifi told media in Wellington.
Rugby.body:
The Hurricanes have stormed their way into the 2026 Super Rugby Pacific as the form team in the competition, scoring over 56 points in both of the last two playoff contests. But as they head into their first chance at Super Rugby Pacific glory since 2016, they only have one player on their current roster who has featured in a final.

Brad Shields, one of their inspirational leaders, is the only player to have played in a Super Rugby Pacific final, starting the Hurricanes’ 2016 triumph over the Lions in the No.6 jersey.

Rugby.title: Jono Gibbes on the Luke Jacobson message that flipped the Chiefs’ season
Rugby.summary: Jacobson, who's played 24 Tests for the All Blacks since making his debut in 2019, addressed his team after their 36-32 defeat on May 22, saying that this won't be the last time they see the Crusaders in 2026.
Rugby.body:
Chiefs head coach Jono Gibbes has credited Luke Jacobson’s leadership for the dominant performance against the Crusaders, where they booked their place in a fourth-straight Super Rugby Pacific final.

Jacobson’s message in the changing room post-defeat at Christchurch’s One New Zealand Stadium in round 15 clearly motivated his side after a disappointing loss. Not only did the Chiefs clinch another final berth on Friday night, but the Hamilton-based franchise did it emphatically, running in six tries during the first half against the Crusaders.

Rugby.title: The seven best performers from the Super Rugby Pacific semi-finals
Rugby.summary: The “Fins Up” celebration was a bit of a "prima donna" moment from a kid who’ll get plenty of attention in the next few seasons.
Rugby.body:
Shortly after the Super Rugby Pacific semi-finals started on Friday night, a fire broke out at Wellington airport. Are the Chiefs too hot to handle a weary traveller, asked. The Crusaders had their 18-game playoff winning streak snapped in a cathartic crushing in Hamilton.

The Chiefs’ 49-12 thrashing of the defending champions was the Crusaders’ biggest losing margin, surpassing the 52-16 loss to the Queensland Reds in Brisbane in 1996. It was 42-7 at halftime in the Chiefs’ first playoff win against the 15-time champions since 2013. In that 20-19 semi-final success, Lelia Masaga scored one of his 38 tries for the Chiefs. Aaron Cruden compiled 15 points, including a try. Daniel Carter converted a try by Israel Dagg and booted four penalties for the Crusaders.

Rugby.title: Vern Cotter opens up on the legacy of his Blues tenure
Rugby.summary: Loose-forward duo Dalton Papali'i and Hoskins Sotutu will both depart, while AJ Lam will join Harry Plummer at Clermont in the French Top 14.
Rugby.body:
Cotter’s Blues fought hard in the first half of the semi-final, and despite trailing by five points at halftime, the Auckland-based franchise believed they were in the contest.

Loose-forward duo Dalton Papali’i and Hoskins Sotutu will both depart, while AJ Lam will join Harry Plummer at Clermont in the French Top 14. Cotter, who has signed with the Queensland Reds from the start of next season, will leave the Blues having won one Super Rugby Pacific title in 2024, and reaching two semi-finals in 2025 and 2026.

Rugby.title: 'Same players but different men'
Rugby.summary: Exeter boss Rob Baxter says his side's rise from their worst season to a Prem final is one of his greatest achievements.
Rugby.body:
Exeter boss Rob Baxter says his side's rise from their worst season to a Prem final is one of his greatest achievements. The Chiefs fought back from 26-10 down to win 27-26 at Bath and book a first final spot in five years.

The victory came a year after the club's worst campaign when they finished ninth in the Prem and suffered the heaviest defeat in their history - a 79-17 loss at Gloucester just over a year ago.

Rugby.title: Rob Baxter reveals surprising half time message in Chiefs' shock win
Rugby.summary: Exeter boss Rob Baxter revealed he was “really confident” his team could produce one of the greatest fightbacks in Gallagher Prem history and leave champions Bath stunned.
Rugby.body:
Exeter boss Rob Baxter revealed he was “really confident” his team could produce one of the greatest fightbacks in Gallagher Prem history and leave champions Bath stunned. The Chiefs wiped out a 16-point interval deficit, scoring 17 unanswered second-half points to win 27-26 and book an appointment with Northampton at Allianz Stadium next Saturday.

It was only the seventh time in 46 Prem play-off games that an away team had triumphed, with replacement prop Ethan Burger’s 68th-minute try leaving Bath down and out and securing Exeter’s first appearance in the final since 2021.

Rugby.title: Exeter fight back to stun Bath and reach Prem final
Rugby.summary: Exeter produce a stunning second-half comeback to narrowly edge defending champions Bath 27-26 and reach the Prem final.
Rugby.body:
Exeter Chiefs produced a sublime second-half turnaround to stun defending champions Bath 27-26 at the Recreation Ground and reach their first Prem final since 2021. The Chiefs became just the second side in the past decade to win a semi-final away from home and will go to the Allianz Stadium next Saturday for the seventh time in their history to face Northampton Saints for the title.

Exeter were 26-10 down at the break but scored 17 unanswered points in the second half as Ben Hammersley, Greg Fisilau and Ethan Burger all crossed as Bath fell apart, and Joe Cokanasiga was sent to the sin-bin.

Rugby.title: Bath player ratings vs Exeter Chiefs | Gallagher PREM 2025/26 semi-finals
Rugby.summary: Bath player ratings: Bath's quest to defend their Gallagher PREM title came to a shuddering end on Saturday as Exeter Chiefs overturned a 26-10 half-time deficit to snatch a dramatic 27-26 victory at the Rec.
Rugby.body:
Bath player ratings: Bath’s quest to defend their Gallagher PREM title came to a shuddering end on Saturday as Exeter Chiefs overturned a 26-10 half-time deficit to snatch a dramatic 27-26 victory at the Rec.

Johann van Graan’s side looked in complete control during a dominant first half, scoring four tries and repeatedly slicing through the Chiefs’ defence. But Exeter’s bench transformed the contest after the break, while Bath struggled to find answers as momentum swung decisively away from them.

Rugby.title: Exeter Chiefs stun champions Bath to book PREM final with Saints
Rugby.summary: Bath’s hopes of booking a third successive Gallagher Prem final appearance in their quest for back-to-back titles ended amid dramatic scenes as Exeter beat them 27-26 at the Recreation Ground.
Rugby.body:
Bath’s hopes of booking a third successive Gallagher Prem final appearance in their quest for back-to-back titles ended amid dramatic scenes as Exeter beat them 27-26 at the Recreation Ground.

The Chiefs wiped out a 16-point interval deficit to score 17 unanswered second-half points and will play Northampton at Allianz Stadium next Saturday. Only three clubs – Wasps, Leicester and Saracens – have won Prem silverware in successive seasons since play-offs were introduced 24 years ago, and membership of that elite group agonisingly eluded Bath.
