Golf.title: 2026 RBC Canadian Open money: Here’s how much every player made
Golf.summary: Check out our list of how much money each player in the field came away with at the 2026 Memorial Tournament played at Muirfield Village. The post 2026 RBC Canadian Open money: Here’s how much every player made appeared first on Golf .
Golf.body:
Just a few years ago, Bud Cauley was a forgotten name. Now he’s right in the throes of PGA Tour competition. Cauley was an amateur star, playing college golf at Alabama with Justin Thomas and became the 7th player to earn enough money on the PGA Tour to earn membership without going to Q-School in 2011.

Success didn’t materialize for Cauley as he found himself struggling to maintain his Tour card before a car crash in 2018 derailed his dream. Cauley didn’t play a professional event between September 2020 and January 2024.

Golf.title: LIV Golf Adelaide 2026 Delivers $69.8 million USD ($97.1 million AUD) Economic Impact for South Australia
Golf.summary: LIV Golf Adelaide 2026 Delivers $69.8 million USD ($97.1 million AUD) Economic Impact for South Australia LIV Golf
Golf.body:
LIV Golf Adelaide 2026 Delivers $69.8 million USD ($97.1 million AUD) Economic Impact for South Australia Largest golf event in Australia’s history yields $118.6 million USD ($165.0 million AUD) in total economic impact; landmark youth golf expansion and enhanced community inclusivity programs extend LIV Golf Adelaide’s benefits to the local communities and younger generations

ADELAIDE and NEW YORK – South Australia Tourism Commission (SATC) and LIV Golf, the global golf league blending world-class competition with entertainment and culture to grow the game worldwide, today announced that the 2026 LIV Golf Adelaide event generated a total economic impact of $118.6 million USD ($165.0 million AUD) to the Australian economy–and delivered $69.8 million USD ($97.1 million AUD) directly into the Adelaide economy, a 36% increase in economic impact compared to the 2025 event. This year’s direct contribution was driven by organizational spending and a significant influx of visitors. The record-breaking event was held at The Grange Golf Club from February 12-15, 2026 and underscores the League’s significant positive influence on its host communities.

Golf.title: Watson finishes solo second at The International Series Morocco
Golf.summary: Watson finishes solo second at The International Series Morocco LIV Golf
Golf.body:
RABAT, Morocco – RangeGoats Golf Club Captain Bubba Watson continued his scorching hot play on Sunday at the International Series Morocco presented by Visit Morocco, but the two-time Masters champion came up one shot shy of winner Taichi Kho of Hong Kong.

Watson shot a 3-under 70 at Royal Golf Dar Es Salam to finish at 18 under for the week while Kho carded a 5-under 68, good enough to claim the trophy at 19 under. Watson, 47, had rounds of 69-66-69 to take a one-shot lead into the final round. On Sunday, he had five birdies and two bogeys.

Golf.title: 5 exercises every golfer should do to improve their golf games
Golf.summary: PGA Tour fitness coach David Sundberg has five exercises that every golfer should do to improve their golf games. The post 5 exercises every golfer should do to improve their golf games appeared first on Golf .
Golf.body:
Everyone has an opinion on golf fitness. Your playing partner swears by the Tuesday spin class. The guy at the range is convinced his rotator cuff routine changed his life. The internet influencer will sell you a 12-week program promising 30 extra yards if you just comment and buy the right resistance band.

Here’s the truth: most golfers are undertrained, moving poorly, and leaving distance, stability, and longevity on the table. This doesn’t happen from a lack of talent, but because nobody ever gave them an honest foundation to build on. The good news is that strength training for golf doesn’t need to be complicated.
Golf.copy:
Everyone has an opinion on golf fitness. Your playing partner swears by the Tuesday spin class. The guy at the range is convinced his rotator cuff routine changed his life. The internet influencer will sell you a 12-week program promising 30 extra yards if you just comment and buy the right resistance band.

Here’s the truth: most golfers are undertrained, moving poorly, and leaving distance, stability, and longevity on the table. This doesn’t happen from a lack of talent, but because nobody ever gave them an honest foundation to build on. The good news is that strength training for golf doesn’t need to be complicated.

Golf.title: One big reason the Titleist GTS lineup takes a big leap from GT
Golf.summary: Titleist GTS made one HUGE improvement over their GT lineup, and it helped our gear editor get into a Titleist driver for the first time. The post One big reason the Titleist GTS lineup takes a big leap from GT appeared first on Golf .
Golf.body:
Two years ago, Titleist launched its GT series of drivers, and the lineup was received extremely well. This year’s GTS drivers take it to another level. GT exploded on the PGA Tour, it won a bunch of tournaments, and the general sentiment was that Titleist was back to being serious about having a lineup of drivers aimed at the true player. No compromises, no frills, just a great lineup of drivers that, if nothing else, were very, very fast. It seemed like everybody was jumping into the GT family.

I never really got along with GT. I had a great fitting at TPI with Hunter, but with no options to bring the weight back in the head on the GT3, and not finding the right feel for the GT2, I was left on an island. It would be obvious to say that the addition of the rear weight in the new GTS3 is what finally sold me back to a Titleist driver, but after testing I actually found out that there’s another hidden feature that could be playing an even bigger role.

Golf.title: Is an emotional connection to your putter holding you back? | Fully Equipped
Golf.summary: On this week's episode of GOLF's Fully Equipped, putting coach Stephen Sweeney explains dives into golfer's connections to their putters. The post Is an emotional connection to your putter holding you back? | Fully Equipped appeared first on Golf .
Golf.body:
Golfers forming emotional attachments with certain clubs is nothing new. Scottie Scheffler has his driver. Ben Hogan was famous for his 1-iron. And Roy McAvoy never missed with his 7-iron.

But the easiest club to become emotionally attached to is the putter. Some golfers use the same putter for years, while others customize them so they represent their personality.

Golf.summary: Phil Mickelson Exposed for ‘Sinister’ Mind Games at First LIV Golf Event heavy.com
Golf.body:
Kevin Van Valkenburg’s recent column for Fried Egg Golf has brought renewed attention to Phil Mickelson’s public image, just days after Golf Digest published allegations involving the six-time major champion and The Farms Golf Club in California.

Mickelson has been away from competitive golf while dealing with what his representatives have described as a family health matter. He will not compete in this week’s U.S. Open at Shinnecock Hills. At the same time, discussion around the LIV Golf figure has intensified following both Golf Digest’s reporting and Van Valkenburg’s personal account of his first encounter with Mickelson at LIV Golf’s inaugural event in 2022.

Golf.title: Brooks Koepka WDs from Canadian Open citing injury
Golf.summary: Brooks Koepka withdrew from the Canadian Open on Sunday citing a hand injury just days before his Shinnecock U.S. Open defense. The post Brooks Koepka WDs from Canadian Open citing injury appeared first on Golf .
Golf.body:
Brooks Koepka’s return to Shinnecock for the first time since winning the 2018 U.S. Open just got a little bit murkier. On Sunday morning, Koepka withdrew from the RBC Canadian Open before his final round citing a hand injury, per the PGA Tour’s communications department. Koepka, who was 6 under and tied for 34th place at the time of the injury, will not have the chance to complete his final round from TPC Toronto, prematurely ending a week that had shown a glimmer of competitive promise for the five-time major champion.

On Saturday afternoon, Koepka indicated he had been dealing with some sort of hand injury during a third round 72 that had seen him slide down the leaderboard after a brilliant opening two days in Toronto.

Golf.title: The story of Shinnecock Hills' first U.S. Open championship in 1896
Golf.summary: What was the first U.S. Open at Shinnecock Hills like in 1896? There was more drama and intrigue than you might imagine. The post The story of Shinnecock Hills’ first U.S. Open championship in 1896 appeared first on Golf .
Golf.body:
This content was first published in Golf Journal, a quarterly print publication exclusively for USGA Members. To be among the first to receive Golf Journal and to learn how you can ensure a strong future for the game, become a USGA Member today!

SOUTHAMPTON, N.Y. (July 18, 1896) – Golf is still in its infancy in the United States, but no one can deny that the game is developing into more than a curiosity. Courses are sprouting, and clubmakers are busy outfitting new players. With every new course laid out on these shores and every man or woman who is intrigued to take up this stick-and-ball pursuit, the Scottish game is becoming ours as well.

Golf.title: Report: PIF has only funded a third of what LIV Golf needs for ’26
Golf.summary: Report: PIF has only funded a third of what LIV Golf needs for ’26 Field Level Media
Golf.body:
Saudi Arabia’s Public Investment Fund, which is pulling funding from LIV Golf after this year, has only provided a third of what the circuit requires to make it through the rest of this season, the Financial Times reported Thursday.

LIV Golf needs $600 million to cover the remainder of the 2026 schedule, per the report. The PIF paid $66 million in early May and $130 million in early June, according to the report.

Golf.title: How hard is it *after* instantly earning your Tour card? This pro knows
Golf.summary: On this week's episode of GOLF's Subpar, Alex Fitzpatrick explains the pressures he felt after earning his PGA Tour card. The post How hard is it *after* instantly earning your Tour card? This pro knows appeared first on Golf .
Golf.body:
Alex Fitzpatrick’s rise to the PGA Tour was well documented and fast. The younger brother of 2022 U.S. Open Champion Matt Fitzpatrick went from winless DP World Tour pro to PGA Tour winner and full-fledged member in the span of four weeks, thanks to his win at the Zurich Classic with Matt.

That put him straight into the field for back-to-back Signature Events in Miami and Charlotte. He didn’t have to keep playing on the DP Tour to try and earn his PGA Tour membership for 2027 and he definitely doesn’t have to go to Q-School.

Golf.title: 9 tips to effortlessly shoot more stress-free low scores
Golf.summary: GOLF Top 100 Teacher Jason Birnbaum shares nine tips you can use to shoot more low scores without even trying. The post 9 tips to effortlessly shoot more stress-free low scores appeared first on Golf .
Golf.body:
Ours is a famously frustrating, stressful game. Like the old joke says: It’s called “golf” because all the other four-letter words were taken. But, #$%!, it doesn’t have to be that way, at least not so often. Maybe the smoothest of the smooth are born that way. The rest of us can still produce our best selves — free flowing — at the best times by following a few simple tips.

Here are nine tips you can use to shoot more effortless low scores. A stress-free round isn’t a trouble-free round. You’re never going to hit every fairway and green. It’s not the bogeys that raise your blood pressure; it’s the angst of trying (and often failing) to pull off difficult recovery shots. Shot choice greatly affects our agita levels: Taking your medicine with a simple pitchout or layup instead of attempting the miraculous keeps golf a walk in the park and doubles and triples off the card.

Golf.title: Why Bryson DeChambeau will be delighted about the USGA's change at Shinnecock ahead of the US Open
Golf.summary: Why Bryson DeChambeau will be delighted about the USGA's change at Shinnecock ahead of the US Open hitc.com
Golf.body:
Bryson DeChambeau has really struggled at the majors this season, but we can never count him out of contending at the very biggest events. DeChambeau winning two US Opens was certainly no fluke.

The 32-year-old American dominated the field at Winged Foot in 2020 and got the better of Rory McIlroy by a single shot at Pinehurst No.2 in 2024. Bryson DeChambeau‘s game shouldn’t be suited to US Open venues, on paper at least.

Golf.title: The PGA Tour's unlikeliest contender had a week you can't believe
Golf.summary: It's possible nobody in all of golf has had a crazier (or more action-packed) week than Ben Kohles, the veteran contending on the PGA tour. The post The PGA Tour’s unlikeliest contender had a week you can’t believe appeared first on Golf .
Golf.body:
Ben Kohles rose six days ago in Greenville, S.C. with a dream. In the next three days, he thought, he’d have ample opportunity to see at least one of his golf dreams realized. Probably through a victory at that afternoon’s BMW Charity Pro-Am, where Kohles awoke with a healthy lead . But maybe through U.S. Open Final Qualifying in Maryland on Monday afternoon, where he was scheduled to play.

The whirlwind that has followed in the last six days has proved so totally disorienting, so remarkable, that Kohles never even considered the possibility he’d wake less than seven days later having achieved both of those dreams. And if that’s true, it’s impossible to believe he saw this weekend coming, where Kohles finds himself contending for his first-ever PGA Tour victory.

Golf.title: What’s up with Tiger & Phil? Questions persist as another major looms
Golf.summary: It’s hard to imagine that both Tiger Woods and Phil Mickelson would all vanish from the sport’s landscape and even its screen-scape. The post What’s up with Tiger & Phil? Questions persist as another major looms appeared first on Golf .
Golf.body:
Now and again, a reader will write in with this existential riddle: What’s up with Tiger and Phil? It’s beyond weird, that golf’s two most dominant figures over the past three decades have all but vanished from the sport’s landscape and even its screen-scape.

The most recent Woods update came courtesy of People magazine. (Telling.) The news was that the 15-time major champion is in a residential treatment facility outside the United States for what appears to be to a three-month stay to address his addiction issues. This return to a rehabilitation center — he sought treatment in a Mississippi facility in 2010 for addictive behavior — came in the wake of his late-March roadside arrest near his home in South Florida. Woods is expected to complete the treatment by the end of June, though he did, per People, return briefly to Florida after his girlfriend, Vanessa Trump, former daughter-in-law of the current president, announced she had breast cancer.

Golf.title: How a 'burnerverse' golfer went from beginner to single-digit handicap in 18 months
Golf.summary: In this edition of Play Smart, we take a look at how a "burnerverse" golfer became a single-digit handicap in just 18 months. The post How a ‘burnerverse’ golfer went from beginner to single-digit handicap in 18 months appeared first on Golf .
Golf.body:
Ed. note: The subject of this story is X user Upper East Side Golf (@UESgolf), a member of the golf “burnerverse.” He is referenced by his username and handle in this article in order to protect his burnerverse identity.

Upper East Side Golf is a certified golf sicko. That term gets thrown around a lot, but there are few that embody the term quite like he does. Each morning, he wakes up around 5:30 a.m. and treks to Five Iron Golf‘s Rockefeller Center location to grind on his game. He often hits balls for 90 minutes and finishes with a lift in the gym before heading to his midtown Manhattan office to start his day job in the financial industry. On mornings when he isn’t grinding on his game in the simulator, you may find him walking the fairways of Pelham Bay golf course in the Bronx.

Golf.title: How Dormie Workshop became one of golf’s most coveted brands
Golf.summary: Love hand-crafted, full-grain leather golf accessories? You’ve likely heard of Dormie Workshop. Here's how the brand gained legions of fans. The post How Dormie Workshop became one of golf’s most coveted brands appeared first on Golf .
Golf.body:
Eight years ago, I was looking for a cool golf gift for my husband, who happens to be a golf pro. Given the fact that he has access to just about everything, finding a unique item that he would actually use but somehow doesn’t already have has always been a real challenge. But at the time, I noticed that there was one glaring deficiency in his golf gear arsenal: a cool headcover. Simple! But I didn’t want to choose just any off-the-rack piece. I wanted his gift to be a custom creation that reflected a part of who he is.

Where could I source such an item? I knew immediately where to go: Dormie Workshop. If you’re a fan of hand-crafted, full-grain leather accessories, chances are you’ve heard of Dormie — a Canadian brand founded by three brothers (Jeff, Todd and Alex Bishop) in 2014.

Golf.title: Never hit a slice again with this simple swing thought
Golf.summary: Struggling with a slice? David Armitage's simple swing cue will help improve your sequencing and straighten out your drives. The post Never hit a slice again with this simple swing thought appeared first on Golf .
Golf.body:
Anyone who’s battled a stubborn slice knows how frustrating it is to watch your drives veer off to the right. Beyond sending your ball into trouble, a slice robs you of both distance and control, turning your driver into one of the most intimidating clubs in your bag. As a result, many amateurs end up benching one of their biggest weapons on the course.

But you don’t have to sacrifice distance to find more fairways. As David Armitage, a GOLF Top 100 Teacher, explains in the video below, all you need is a simple swing cue to improve your sequencing and straighten out your drives.

Golf.title: Rahm Chases DeChambeau at LIV Golf Event
Golf.summary: Rahm Chases DeChambeau at LIV Golf Event golfpost.com
Golf.body:
Jon Rahm faces an uphill battle at a LIV Golf event, trailing Bryson DeChambeau by seven shots at the halfway stage. Despite the significant gap on the leaderboard, the Spanish golfer demonstrated the competitive spirit expected of a champion during his fourth shot of a particular hole.

Commentary during the broadcast highlighted Rahm's power and determination as he worked his way through the course. The observation that "it's what champions do" underscored his ability to maintain composure and aggression even when facing a substantial deficit against DeChambeau, who currently holds the lead.

Golf.title: 2026 RBC Canadian Open Saturday tee times: Round 3 pairings
Golf.summary: Full 2026 RBC Canadian Open tee times for Saturday's third round at TPC Toronto, featuring Sam Burns, Brooks Koepka and more. The post 2026 RBC Canadian Open Saturday tee times: Round 3 pairings appeared first on Golf .
Golf.body:
The 2026 RBC Canadian Open continues on Saturday with the third round at TPC Toronto. You can find full RBC Canadian Open tee times for Saturday’s third round at the bottom of this post.

After firing a second-round 63, American Ben James leads the charge at the Canadian Open’s halfway point with a 36-hole score of 10 under par. The 23-year-old just completed his senior season at the University of Virginia and is riding some incredible momentum. The RBC Canadian Open marks James’ pro debut after earning his Tour card by finishing first in the PGA Tour University Ranking, and he’s leading the tournament by one shot after also qualifying for his third U.S. Open on Monday this week.

Golf.title: Club revokes Mickelson membership over allegations
Golf.summary: Phil Mickelson's membership at Farms Golf Club has been revoked amid allegations of inappropriate contact with a female employee at the Californian establishment.
Golf.body:
Six-time major winner Phil Mickelson's membership at a high-end golf club has been revoked amid allegations of inappropriate contact with a female employee at the Californian establishment.

The incident is alleged to have occurred earlier this spring and following a complaint by the employee Mickelson was asked to leave mid-round while playing at the Farms Golf Club in Rancho Santa Fe near San Diego.

Golf.title: Viktor Hovland brought 1 driver to Canada. It wasn't his gamer | Tour Report
Golf.summary: In this week's Tour Report, Viktor Hovland makes a major driver switch in what was otherwise a light week on the gear front. The post Viktor Hovland brought 1 driver to Canada. It wasn’t his gamer | Tour Report appeared first on Golf .
Golf.body:
Welcome to Fully Equipped’s weekly Tour equipment report. Every Friday of PGA Tour weeks (plus other times, if news warrants), GOLF equipment editor Jack Hirsh runs you through some of the biggest news surrounding golf clubs on Tour, including changes, tweaks and launches.

Viktor Hovland showed up to this week’s RBC Canadian Open with one driver. Normally, that wouldn’t be much of a story, but the driver Hovland brought wasn’t the same one he used in his last start at the PGA Championship or for the last six years.

Golf.title: Ping's PLD Putting Lab showed me something I hadn't seen before
Golf.summary: A trip to Ping Golf's PLD Putting Lab showed me a whole new world for putter development. The post Ping’s PLD Putting Lab showed me something I hadn’t seen before appeared first on Golf .
Golf.body:
I recently took a trip to the Ping PLD Putting Lab in Phoenix, AZ and went through an exploration of their new Scottsdale TEC putter lineup, including wearing some Tobii Eye Tracking glasses to learn more about their new Eye Q alignment features.

Along with learning about their new putters and the research into ‘Quiet Eye Theory’ that went along with them, I took away a couple of key things for my putting. I’ve said it before, and I will continue to say it: Ping is never anything short of impressive, and the tools they use and share for golfers always unlock new knowledge.

Golf.title: Top PGA Tour Prize Funds Not Expected To Decrease Regardless Of LIV Golf's Fate
Golf.summary: Top PGA Tour Prize Funds Not Expected To Decrease Regardless Of LIV Golf's Fate Golf Monthly
Golf.body:
PGA Tour prize money in the biggest events is not expected to return to former levels, regardless of what happens to the LIV Golf League, Golf Monthly understands. The PGA Tour introduced consistent $20 million prize money payouts back in 2022 in response to LIV Golf being born, having previously operated four $12 million tournaments as its most lucrative events outside of the Majors, The Players Championship and FedEx Cup Playoffs.

Since 2022, there have been at least eight $20 million Signature Events during every regular season, making up a total purse of around half a billion dollars each year if the Majors, FedEx Cup Playoffs and FedEx Cup Fall are all taken into account as well.

Golf.title: The Mizuno Pro Fli-Hi might be my favorite driving iron
Golf.summary: The Mizuno Pro Fli-Hi has been a favorite for golfers for many years, so how did they make the new one even better? The post The Mizuno Pro Fli-Hi might be my favorite driving iron appeared first on Golf .
Golf.body:
When I visited The Foundry, Mizuno Golf’s R&D and Fitting headquarters just outside of Atlanta, I picked up one of their new Pro Fli-Hi utility irons. It was already built in the fitting bay with a Ventus Black HB shaft. I essentially fell (back) in love with the club.

There’s just something about the Fli-Hi that makes it one of my favorite utility irons on the market, if not my No. 1 favorite. I spent some time on the course with the Mizuno team hitting it into a tricky par-5, and it certainly showed off its abilities.

Badminton.title: BlackWall Limited Director Seph Glew Acquires Additional BWF Shares On-Market
Badminton.summary: BlackWall Limited Director Seph Glew Acquires Additional BWF Shares On-Market Kalkine
Badminton.body:
BlackWall Limited (ASX: BWF) has disclosed a change in the relevant interests of director JR (Seph) Glew, following an on-market Acquisition of 7,437 ordinary shares on 12 June 2026. The purchase was made through Seno Management Pty Ltd as Trustee for the Taipa Account, an entity in which Glew is both a director of the trustee and a beneficiary of the trust. The transaction, valued at $1,078.37, brings Glew's total holding in BlackWall Limited to 52,165,690 BWF ordinary shares. For investors, director buying activity — even at modest dollar values — is often interpreted as a signal of confidence in the company's prospects.

BlackWall Limited notified the market on 12 June 2026 that director JR (Seph) Glew had acquired 7,437 ordinary shares in the company through an on-market transaction. The shares were purchased at a total consideration of $1,078.37, implying an average acquisition price of approximately $0.145 per share, though the company did not explicitly disclose the per-share price in the notice. The transaction was executed through Seno Management Pty Ltd operating in its capacity as trustee for the Taipa Account.

Badminton.title: Humans of Shuttle Time: Alžbeta Peruňská
Badminton.summary: Humans of Shuttle Time: Alžbeta Peruňská BWF
Badminton.body:
This is the 69th story in our Humans of Shuttle Time series, in which we present the perspectives of those who are involved with badminton at the grassroots level. Alžbeta Peruňská, Shuttle Time Tutor with Slovak Badminton Federation, talks of her eventful journey in badminton.

I’ve lived in Bratislava, capital of Slovakia, since I was born. I had a very nice childhood, my parents always took care of me and spent quality time with me and my older sister watching or reading fairytales, talking about our dreams, and playing table games together. My parents led me to be active and introduced me to different sports before I chose to concentrate on badminton. I chose the school where I wanted to study, and my parents have always supported me.

Badminton.title: Members’ Exceptional Achievements Honoured
Badminton.summary: Members’ Exceptional Achievements Honoured BWF
Badminton.body:
BWF recognised outstanding Member achievements at the fifth edition of the BWF Member Association Awards announced at the 87th BWF AGM in Horsens, Denmark. Each recipient received a US$ 2,500 equipment grant.

The Argentine Badminton Federation was recognised for ‘Proyección 2036’, a national grassroots mini-badminton initiative launched in 2024. Targeting Under-9 and Under-11 players with a long-term Olympic development vision, the programme has expanded across six provinces and eight localities, creating a structured and inclusive pathway that combines technical training, education and community engagement.

Badminton.title: David Turner Honoured with BWF Meritorious Service Award
Badminton.summary: David Turner Honoured with BWF Meritorious Service Award Badminton Oceania
Badminton.body:
SYDNEY, AUSTRALIA — Badminton Oceania is proud to celebrate one of our region’s most dedicated servants, David Turner, who was presented with the Badminton World Federation Meritorious Service Award on Sunday, 14 June at the SATHIO GROUP Australian Badminton Open in Sydney Olympic Park.

The BWF Meritorious Service Award is one of the highest honours in international badminton, recognising individuals whose dedication, leadership and commitment have made a lasting contribution to the sport. David was nominated by Badminton Australia in recognition of his extraordinary service to badminton in Australia — a career spanning more than five decades that has also left a significant mark on Badminton Oceania and the wider international community.

David was nominated by Badminton Australia in recognition of his extraordinary service to badminton in Australia — a career spanning more than five decades that has also left a significant mark on Badminton Oceania and the wider international community.

Badminton.title: All set for the second edition of the Czech International Future Series
Badminton.summary: All set for the second edition of the Czech International Future Series Badminton Europe
Badminton.body:
The European Circuit goes from France to Czechia this week for the Czech International Future Series 2026. The tournament returns for its second edition after a successful first.

The tournament kicks off on Thursday 18 June and takes place until Sunday 21 June, and all matches will be streamed live on BadmintonEurope.tv. The draw has a good mix of international and European players, and some of Czechia's strongest homegrown talents. In men's singles, Austria's Kai Niederhuber will be the second seed, while Czech player Dominik Kopřiva is seeded fifth.

Badminton.title: Lin Dan Joins Usain Bolt in Ambassadorial Role
Badminton.summary: Lin Dan Joins Usain Bolt in Ambassadorial Role BWF
Badminton.body:
Badminton great Lin Dan visited Serbia last week as part of promotional activities for EXPO 2027 Belgrade, where he was officially presented as an ambassador for the global event.

The two-time Olympic gold medallist and five-time world champion joined a growing group of EXPO 2027 ambassadors that includes sprinting legend Usain Bolt and film star Jackie Chan. During his visit, Lin Dan participated in an on-court session with Serbian players at the National Badminton Centre.

Badminton.title: Macau Open: Jiang, Wei Resume Partnership
Badminton.summary: Macau Open: Jiang, Wei Resume Partnership BWF World Tour
Badminton.body:
World No.2 mixed pair Jiang Zhen Bang and Wei Ya Xin will play their first match together in three months at SANDS CHINA LTD. Macau Open 2026. Following their quarterfinal exit at the All England, Jiang/Wei withdrew from the Badminton Asia Championships. Wei subsequently partnered Gao Jia Xuan at four tournaments, claiming the Malaysia Masters title in Kuala Lumpur last month.

The Macau Open will be Jiang/Wei’s fifth tournament of the season. Their best result so far was a runners-up finish at the Malaysia Open in January, while their most recent title together came at the Arctic Open last October.

Badminton.title: Axelsen withdraws from 2025 BWF World Tour season
Badminton.summary: Axelsen withdraws from 2025 BWF World Tour season SpoTV
Badminton.body:
Viktor Axelsen announced that he would not be competing in the remaining 2025 BWF World Tour tournaments to focus on preparing for the 2026 season. The two-time Olympic champion shared the news on Friday via his official X account.

“I won’t be able to compete [at the Japan Masters] and [Australian Open] as originally planned,” he wrote. “I am doing everything I can to get my body ready for 2026 [and I am] blessed to have good people around me who [are] supporting me in the best way possible.”

Badminton.title: Australian Open: Feng/Huang Rediscover Winning Touch
Badminton.summary: Australian Open: Feng/Huang Rediscover Winning Touch BWF World Tour
Badminton.body:
For five months, Feng Yan Zhe and Huang Dong Ping had been searching for a spark. The Chinese mixed pair finally found it on Sunday, at SATHIO GROUP Australian Open 2026, defeating compatriots Guo Xin Wa and Chen Fang Hui 21-17 21-19 to reclaim a title they won in 2023.

The victory not only extended Feng and Huang’s perfect record against Guo and Chen to seven matches, it also ended one of their partnership’s longest droughts. Since bagging the Malaysia Open in January, the pair endured an unusually barren stretch, failing to reach the final at five consecutive tournaments and suffering defeats to unseeded opponents at the Singapore and Indonesia Opens.

Badminton.title: Sindhu, Satwik-Chirag to lead India's charge at Asian Games
Badminton.summary: Sindhu, Satwik-Chirag to lead India's charge at Asian Games The New Indian Express
Badminton.body:
CHENNAI: Two-time Asian Games medallist PV Sindhu, Asiad men's doubles gold medallists Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty will lead India's charge in the upcoming Asian Games, which is set to be held in Aichi-Nagoya (Japan) from September 19 to October 4.

The Badminton Association of India (BAI) selection committee finalized the squad after reviewing recent performances across national and international tournaments, alongside BWF rankings as of May 26, 2026.

Badminton.title: Australian Open: Relentless Yamaguchi in Fourth Straight Final
Badminton.summary: Australian Open: Relentless Yamaguchi in Fourth Straight Final BWF World Tour
Badminton.body:
Akane Yamaguchi’s remarkable month-long surge shows no signs of slowing as the Japanese top seed booked her fourth consecutive final with a 22-20 21-12 victory over Pusarla V. Sindhu at SATHIO GROUP Australian Open 2026 on Saturday.

The three-time world champion, who lifted the Thailand Open title four weeks ago before finishing runner-up to world No.1 An Se Young at the Singapore and Indonesia Opens, continued her impressive form in Sydney without dropping a game all week. Her latest win also extended an unbeaten streak against Pusarla dating back to 2022.

Competitor News.title: CONFIRMED: Real Madrid sign Marc Cucurella on six year deal
Competitor News.summary: CONFIRMED: Real Madrid sign Marc Cucurella on six year deal Citi Sports Online
Competitor News.body:
Spanish La Liga side Real Madrid has officially signed Spanish left back Marc Cucurella on a six year deal from English Premier League side Chelsea FC. The move was confirmed on Monday, June 15 by Real Madrid after reports surfaced of the club’s interest in the former Brighton and Hove Albion player.

“Real Madrid C. F. and Chelsea FC have reached an agreement for the transfer of the player Marc Cucurella, who will be linked to our club for the next six seasons, until June 30, 2032” the club stated.
